mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and yields positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive 60-day return in more than 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a supply-constrained environment reflects capacity utilization bottlenecks that force firms to raise prices, triggering inflation fears and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 65% of periods where industrial production accelerates within the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 270, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces immediate negative S&P 500 adjustments over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations where CPIAUCSL surpasses the 270 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect shows a negative rank IC in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger economic output and corporate revenue growth, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 drops below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of high-GDP periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below 95 during a recessionary phase signals severe output collapse and falling corporate profits, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where industrial production falls below 95 during the specified regime.
